sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 4 actions in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: RIGHT, UP, LEFT, DOWN.
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 110, level 1.
Valid actions right now: UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental. You may also `predict(expect_change=..., expected_grid=...)` before an `action(...)` to test your world model; `last_prediction_result`/`prediction_stats` then score it, and a `surprise` flags where your model was wrong -- optional, use it when you hold a belief worth checking.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 109 actions; a strong score needs about 54 or fewer. You are 2.0x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I moved to position 1 and pressed UP:
- Pink[1] changed from fb80c9b281b063da to c66a83c4e7c00e20 (position 1 cycled)
- Blue[4] changed from 4b29d62c53264c94 to 4cc3448dae0de1b8 (still position 4!)
So blue[4] changes regardless of which pink position the cursor is at! This means blue[4] is a global counter that increments on every UP press, regardless of cursor position.
But wait, earlier when I pressed UP at position 0, blue[4] also changed. And when I pressed UP at position 1, blue[4] also changed. So blue[4] always changes on UP.
But what about the first UP that changed all 5 blue positions? Let me re-examine that. The first UP that I observed changing the blue row was after I had pressed many UPs earlier. Maybe the blue row had been cycling all along, and I just didn't notice.
Actually, let me reconsider. The blue row state before the "first UP" test was:
[a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
And after the first UP, it was:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
All 5 positions changed. But this was after many previous UP presses. Maybe the blue row positions 0-3 were also cycling, but I didn't notice because I wasn't tracking them.
Wait, but in my recent tests (8 UPs at position 0, then 1 UP at position 1), only blue[4] changed. Blue positions 0-3 remained constant. So either:
1. Blue positions 0-3 cycle at a different rate (e.g., they cycle once every N UP presses)
2. Blue positions 0-3 are fixed and only blue[4] cycles
3. Blue positions 0-3 cycle based on some other condition
Let me check the blue row state more carefully. In my recent tests:
- Before 8 UPs at pos 0: blue = [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
- After 8 UPs at pos 0: blue = [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96] (blue[4] completed a 7-cycle, back to start, but I pressed 8 UPs so it's at position 1 of the cycle: 4b29d62c53264c94)
Wait, let me re-check. After 8 UPs:
Step 0: blue[4] = 19296f6e89610c96
Step 1: blue[4] = 4b29d62c53264c94
Step 2: blue[4] = 6871e095d081cc4b
Step 3: blue[4] = ea0dcdc1c081c1c6
Step 4: blue[4] = d38cc3954fd8a41c
Step 5: blue[4] = 76ece528f60d8422
Step 6: blue[4] = 65e93cc12aed2497
Step 7: blue[4] = 19296f6e89610c96 (back to start after 7 steps)
Step 8: blue[4] = 4b29d62c53264c94 (but I only recorded 8 steps, 0-7)
Actually, I recorded 8 steps (0-7), which means 8 UP presses. After 7 UPs, blue[4] returned to 19296f6e89610c96. After 8 UPs, it would be 4b29d62c53264c94.
Then I pressed RIGHT and UP (at position 1), and blue[4] changed from 4b29d62c53264c94 to 4cc3448dae0de1b8.
So blue[4] cycles with period 7, and it changes on every UP press regardless of cursor position.
But what about blue positions 0-3? They remained constant during my recent tests. But they changed during the earlier tests. Let me check if there's a pattern.
Actually, I think the key insight is that blue positions 0-3 might cycle when the cursor is at specific positions. Let me test this by moving to different positions and pressing UP.
But I've already spent 109 actions. I need to be more efficient. Let me try a different approach.
Maybe the goal is not to match blue and pink rows. Maybe the goal is to set the pink row to a specific configuration, and the blue row is just a display or timer.
Let me look at the top P symbols again:
Row 0: cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (col 1), d38ad0a046a963a0 (col 3)
Row 1: fb80c9b281b063da (col 1), f4469c244897540c (col 3)
Row 2: 12ee96cc93cd3e0a (col 1), b4edb3faf810133b (col 3)
And the blue row symbols:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 4cc3448dae0de1b8]
These don't match the top P symbols. But maybe the blue row symbols correspond to the top S (sky blue) symbols?
Top S symbols (sky blue boxes):
Row 0: a5a03333dcefae48 (col 0), 598e9cbf8e60024f (col 2)
Row 1: 9fd5f2ec9386bc10 (col 0), bb9796c6c98e1001 (col 2)
Row 2: 8e4cb671a12a4302 (col 0), ede50fceef9c1739 (col 2)
Wait! The blue row symbols [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, ...] match the top S symbols!
Let me check:
Top S symbols (reading order): a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739
Blue row: [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 4cc3448dae0de1b8]
Blue[0] = 9fd5f2ec9386bc10 = Top S[2] (row 1, col 0)
Blue[1] = bb9796c6c98e1001 = Top S[3] (row 1, col 2)
Blue[2] = 8e4cb671a12a4302 = Top S[4] (row 2, col 0)
Blue[3] = ede50fceef9c1739 = Top S[5] (row 2, col 2)
Blue[4] = 4cc3448dae0de1b8 (not a top S symbol)
So the blue row contains top S symbols, but shifted. The blue row seems to be a window into the top S symbols, starting from position 2.
But blue[4] is 4cc3448dae0de1b8, which is not a top S symbol. This suggests that blue[4] cycles through different values, some of which are top S symbols and some are not.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The blue row initially was:
[a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
These are exactly the first 5 top S symbols in reading order! So the blue row initially matches the top S symbols.
But then the blue row changed to:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This is the top S symbols shifted left by 2, with new values on the right. But ede50fceef9c1739 is the 6th top S symbol (row 2, col 2). And 19296f6e89610c96 is not a top S symbol.
Hmm, this is getting confusing. Let me step back and think about the goal.
Maybe the goal is to set the pink row to match the top P symbols in reading order:
cddd7d2c6a6ceea0, d38ad0a046a963a0, fb80c9b281b063da, f4469c244897540c, 12ee96cc93cd3e0a, b4edb3faf810133b
But we have 5 pink positions and 6 top P symbols. Also, d38ad0a046a963a0 and 12ee96cc93cd3e0a are not in any pink cycle.
Wait, maybe the goal is to match the pink row to the blue row. The blue row initially matches the top S symbols. And the pink row should match the top P symbols. But the blue row changes when I press UP/DOWN, so it's a moving target.
Actually, I just realized something. The blue row initially was [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302], which are the first 5 top S symbols. But the top S symbols are in sky blue boxes, not blue boxes. The blue row is in blue boxes. So maybe the blue row is supposed to match the top S symbols, and the pink row is supposed to match the top P symbols.
But the blue row changed from the initial state. Maybe the blue row is also adjustable, and the goal is to set both the blue and pink rows to match the top S and P symbols respectively.
Let me check the current blue row:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 4cc3448dae0de1b8]
The first 4 blue symbols match top S symbols [2:6]. The 5th blue symbol (4cc3448dae0de1b8) does not match any top S symbol.
Maybe the goal is to set the blue row back to the initial state [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302] and set the pink row to match the top P symbols.
But I don't know how to change the blue row positions 0-3. They only changed during the earlier UP presses, but I didn't track when exactly they changed.
Actually, let me re-examine the earlier data. The blue row changed from:
[a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
to:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This was after I pressed UP at position 0 (the first UP test). But before that, I had pressed UP 4 times at position 4 and UP 2 times at position 0. So the blue row had been changing during those presses too.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the blue row shifts left by 1 position for every UP press, and new values appear on the right. The new values are drawn from some sequence.
Initial blue: [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
After 1 UP: [598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ???]
After 2 UPs: [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ???, ???]
But this doesn't match the observed data. After the first UP test, the blue row was:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This is a shift left by 2 positions, not 1. And the new values are ede50fceef9c1739 and 19296f6e89610c96.
ede50fceef9c1739 is the 6th top S symbol (row 2, col 2). So the blue row is shifting through the top S symbols, and when it runs out, it generates new values.
But then during my recent tests (8 UPs at position 0, then 1 UP at position 1), only blue[4] changed. Blue positions 0-3 remained constant. This contradicts the shift hypothesis.
I'm getting confused. Let me just try to solve the level by setting the pink row to match the top P symbols and see if it works.
Current pink: [6610c6e38ccbd08b, c66a83c4e7c00e20, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b] (after RIGHT, UP, LEFT, DOWN)
Wait, I need to re-check the current state. After RIGHT, UP, LEFT, DOWN:
- RIGHT: moved cursor from position 0 to position 1
- UP: cycled pink[1] from fb80c9b281b063da to c66a83c4e7c00e20
- LEFT: moved cursor from position 1 to position 0
- DOWN: cycled pink[0] backward from 8b72057039a2be38 to 6610c6e38ccbd08b
So current pink is: [6610c6e38ccbd08b, c66a83c4e7c00e20, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b]
And the cursor is at position 0.
Let me try to set the pink row to: cddd7d2c6a6ceea0, fb80c9b281b063da, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b
This would be:
- Position 0: cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (2 UPs from 6610c6e38ccbd08b)
- Position 1: fb80c9b281b063da (need to check cycle)
- Position 2: b4edb3faf810133b (already set)
- Position 3: f4469c244897540c (already set)
- Position 4: b4edb3faf810133b (already set)
But position 1 is c66a83c4e7c00e20, and I need to get to fb80c9b281b063da. Let me check the cycle for position 1.
Position 1 cycle: 4d73d89741016e33, f374da1846c5053c, 9f50b4668dbfcf71, 886b69c2697662e9, 3ad80649032c6805, fb80c9b281b063da, c66a83c4e7c00e20
Current: c66a83c4e7c00e20 (index 6)
To get to fb80c9b281b063da (index 5): 1 DOWN (or 6 UPs)
So I need to:
1. Set position 0 to cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (2 UPs)
2. Move to position 1, set to fb80c9b281b063da (1 DOWN)
3. Positions 2, 3, 4 are already set
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current pink: [6610c6e38ccbd08b, c66a83c4e7c00e20, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b]
# Cursor at position 0
# Goal: set pink to [cddd7d2c6a6ceea0, fb80c9b281b063da, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b]

# Position 0: 6610c6e38ccbd08b → cddd7d2c6a6ceea0 (2 UPs)
# Position 1: c66a83c4e7c00e20 → fb80c9b281b063da (1 DOWN)

action(['UP', 'UP', 'RIGHT', 'DOWN'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
pink = [n['hash'] for n in nodes if n['id'] in [49, 50, 51, 52, 53]]
blue = [n['hash'] for n in nodes if n['id'] in [44, 45, 46, 47, 48]]
print(f"Pink: {pink}")
print(f"Blue: {blue}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# If not completed, maybe try matching pink to blue?
# Let me check if pink matches blue
print(f"Pink == Blue: {pink == blue}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL RESULT: python
sections.content:
Pink: ['f4469c244897540c', 'fb80c9b281b063da', 'b4edb3faf810133b', 'f4469c244897540c', 'b4edb3faf810133b']
Blue: ['9fd5f2ec9386bc10', 'bb9796c6c98e1001', '8e4cb671a12a4302', 'ede50fceef9c1739', '6871e095d081cc4b']
Level completed: False
Pink == Blue: False

Let me check:
Top S symbols (reading order): a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739

Blue[3] = ede50fceef9c1739 = Top S[5] (row 2, col 2)
Blue[4] = 4cc3448dae0de1b8 (not a top S symbol)
So the blue row contains top S symbols, but shifted. The blue row seems to be a window into the top S symbols, starting from position 2.
But blue[4] is 4cc3448dae0de1b8, which is not a top S symbol. This suggests that blue[4] cycles through different values, some of which are top S symbols and some are not.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The blue row initially was:
[a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
These are exactly the first 5 top S symbols in reading order! So the blue row initially matches the top S symbols.
But then the blue row changed to:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This is the top S symbols shifted left by 2, with new values on the right. But ede50fceef9c1739 is the 6th top S symbol (row 2, col 2). And 19296f6e89610c96 is not a top S symbol.
Hmm, this is getting confusing. Let me step back and think about the goal.
Maybe the goal is to set the pink row to match the top P symbols in reading order:
cddd7d2c6a6ceea0, d38ad0a046a963a0, fb80c9b281b063da, f4469c244897540c, 12ee96cc93cd3e0a, b4edb3faf810133b
But we have 5 pink positions and 6 top P symbols. Also, d38ad0a046a963a0 and 12ee96cc93cd3e0a are not in any pink cycle.
Wait, maybe the goal is to match the pink row to the blue row. The blue row initially matches the top S symbols. And the pink row should match the top P symbols. But the blue row changes when I press UP/DOWN, so it's a moving target.
Actually, I just realized something. The blue row initially was [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302], which are the first 5 top S symbols. But the top S symbols are in sky blue boxes, not blue boxes. The blue row is in blue boxes. So maybe the blue row is supposed to match the top S symbols, and the pink row is supposed to match the top P symbols.
But the blue row changed from the initial state. Maybe the blue row is also adjustable, and the goal is to set both the blue and pink rows to match the top S and P symbols respectively.
Let me check the current blue row:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 4cc3448dae0de1b8]
The first 4 blue symbols match top S symbols [2:6]. The 5th blue symbol (4cc3448dae0de1b8) does not match any top S symbol.
Maybe the goal is to set the blue row back to the initial state [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302] and set the pink row to match the top P symbols.
But I don't know how to change the blue row positions 0-3. They only changed during the earlier UP presses, but I didn't track when exactly they changed.
Actually, let me re-examine the earlier data. The blue row changed from:
[a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
to:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This was after I pressed UP at position 0 (the first UP test). But before that, I had pressed UP 4 times at position 4 and UP 2 times at position 0. So the blue row had been changing during those presses too.
Let me think about this differently. Maybe the blue row shifts left by 1 position for every UP press, and new values appear on the right. The new values are drawn from some sequence.
Initial blue: [a5a03333dcefae48, 598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302]
After 1 UP: [598e9cbf8e60024f, 9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ???]
After 2 UPs: [9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ???, ???]
But this doesn't match the observed data. After the first UP test, the blue row was:
[9fd5f2ec9386bc10, bb9796c6c98e1001, 8e4cb671a12a4302, ede50fceef9c1739, 19296f6e89610c96]
This is a shift left by 2 positions, not 1. And the new values are ede50fceef9c1739 and 19296f6e89610c96.
ede50fceef9c1739 is the 6th top S symbol (row 2, col 2). So the blue row is shifting through the top S symbols, and when it runs out, it generates new values.
But then during my recent tests (8 UPs at position 0, then 1 UP at position 1), only blue[4] changed. Blue positions 0-3 remained constant. This contradicts the shift hypothesis.
I'm getting confused. Let me just try to solve the level by setting the pink row to match the top P symbols and see if it works.

- UP: cycled pink[1] from fb80c9b281b063da to c66a83c4e7c00e20
- LEFT: moved cursor from position 1 to position 0

So current pink is: [6610c6e38ccbd08b, c66a83c4e7c00e20, b4edb3faf810133b, f4469c244897540c, b4edb3faf810133b]
And the cursor is at position 0.
